episode_summary: A live episode features four facts spanning an English field historically named “Disneyland,” Japan’s annual anti-Valentine march by the Revolutionary Alliance of Unpopular Men, a WWII battleship whose gun barrels were cleaned using a priest as a human pipe cleaner, and the Washington Post’s attempts to interview President Taft’s pet cow. Along the way the hosts riff on Disney trivia, farming and irrigation, Valentine customs, eccentric clergy, and presidential pets.

facts.fact: My fact this week is that there was a Disneyland in England 569 years before there was one in America.
My fact this week is that every year in Japan, there is an annual anti-Valentine march held by a group known as the Revolutionary Alliance of Unpopular Men.

Okay, my fact this week is that during World War II, the guns of the ship the HMS Queen Elizabeth were cleaned by wrapping a priest in a large cloth and pulling him through the barrels like a human pipe cleaner.
My fact is that between 1910 and 1912, the Washington Post frequently tried to interview the president's pet cow.
